title: Require MFA in an IAM Policy
description:
## Gate Sensitive Actions Behind MFA

A strong control is to allow privileged actions **only when the caller authenticated with MFA**, using the `aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent` condition.

**Your task:** write an IAM policy at **`/root/mfa-policy.json`** with a statement whose **`Condition`** requires **`aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent`** to be **`true`** (e.g. an Allow gated on MFA, or a Deny when MFA is absent).

> 💡 `"Condition": { "Bool": { "aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent": "true" } }` on an Allow, or `"BoolIfExists": { "aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent": "false" }` on a Deny.

hints.title: MFA-gated Allow
hints.body: Add a Bool condition on aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent so the statement only applies to MFA-authenticated sessions.

validation.description: Checks that a statement is conditioned on aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent.

commands.description: A statement requires MFA via aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent
commands.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/root/mfa-policy.json')); s=d['Statement']; s=s if isinstance(s,list) else [s]; ok=any('multifactorauthpresent' in json.dumps(st.get('Condition',{})).lower() for st in s); print('ok' if ok else 'no')"
